title: Bauhinia cuyabensis
taxonomy: Bauhinia cuyabensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes or peas. It belongs to the genus Bauhinia and is native to Central and South America. The plant is part of the tribe Cercideae and is closely related to other Bauhinia species found in the tropical regions of the Americas. Bauhinia cuyabensis is characterized by its distinctive flowers and leaves, making it a unique addition to the Fabaceae family. The plant's classification is further supported by molecular studies, which have confirmed its place within the Cercideae tribe.

The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, inflammatory diseases, and gastrointestinal disorders, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
